name: Humacyte, Inc.
business: Humacyte, Inc. is a commercial-stage biotechnology platform company developing universally implantable, bioengineered human tissues at commercial scale, and in the first quarter of 2025 commenced the United States commercial launch of our first FDA-approved product, Symvess (acellular tissue engineered vessel-tyod), for use in adults as a vascular conduit for extremity arterial injury when urgent revascularization is needed to avoid imminent limb loss, and autologous vein graft is not feasible.

exposures.sentence: Humacyte, Inc. is a commercial-stage biotechnology platform company developing universally implantable, bioengineered human tissues at commercial scale, and in the first quarter of 2025 commenced the United States commercial launch of our first FDA-approved product.

Humacyte has a strong working relationship with the Department of Defense (DoD) that has led to a partnership over the last decade to support their unmet need to reconstruct and repair vascular injuries through the development of our ATEVs.

catalysts.event: FDA approval of Symvess for extremity vascular trauma
catalysts.sentence: On December 19, 2024, the FDA granted full approval for the ATEV under the brand name Symvess for use in adults as a vascular conduit for extremity arterial injury when urgent revascularization is needed to avoid imminent limb loss, and autologous vein graft is not feasible.
in the first quarter of 2025 commenced the United States commercial launch of our first FDA-approved product.
We plan to utilize the collective preclinical data on the sdATEV to support an Investigational New Drug (IND) application to the FDA for CABG during 2025.

catalysts.event: Interim analysis of V012 Phase 3 trial in AV access for hemodialysis
catalysts.sentence: The 80th patient is expected to complete one year of follow up in April 2026.

catalysts.event: Supplemental BLA submission for AV access for hemodialysis
catalysts.sentence: Dependent upon interim results from our ongoing V012 Phase 3 trial of the ATEV for use in AV access for hemodialysis in women, we plan to submit a supplemental BLA for the ATEV to the FDA for an indication in AV access for hemodialysis in the second half of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 26,000 patients per year are eligible for ATEV in the United States for extremity vascular trauma.
tamClaims.sentence: Based on an analysis of the Definitive Healthcare Claims (DHC) Database 2023, we estimate that approximately 26,000 patients per year will be eligible for the ATEV within the United States

tamClaims.claim: Annually, at least 160,000 existing or new dialysis patients require a new AV access in the U.S. and an additional 150,000 patients require a new AV access in Europe and Japan.
tamClaims.sentence: Annually, at least 160,000 existing or new dialysis patients require a new AV access in the U.S. and an additional 150,000 patients require a new AV access in Europe and Japan.

tamClaims.claim: Over 230,000 PAD-related procedures are reported annually in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: Peripheral arterial bypass procedures are common with over 230,000 PAD-related procedures reported annually in the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: More than 400,000 CABG procedures reported annually in the U.S. and over 800,000 annual CABG procedures globally.
tamClaims.sentence: CABG procedures are common, with more than 400,000 CABG procedures reported annually in the U.S. and over 800,000 annual CABG procedures globally.
